generatedSentence: Elara, once a sharp agent, now lived a spiffy but mundane life as a taxpayer. Her days, though often filled with jokes at the juniper bar, felt somewhat plain. Her only dream was to finish her novel. An electronic chime signaled an emailed update on a trivial topic. She was unpacking old memories, feeling like an umpire in her own life, a solitary, jolly gnome-like figure, though she considered herself the luckiest for her peace. Suddenly, a chilling message arrived, encrypted with ancient sigils, speaking of her old nemesis. He was rising from the nether realms, his mind more twisted than ever. He threatened Elara's family, aiming to unleash chaos from a forgotten factory near the ancient tombstones. Elara felt upset, a deep umbra consuming her as she saw his drunken demonic influence spread. She had to be defensive, navigating a mucky trail of gravel to stop his deliciously cruel plot. Elara, fueled by faith, engaged in a fierce firefight on the mountain slopes. Enemy sharpshooters and snipers unleashed dark sorcery. The Joker, her tormentor, watched from a hidden jail. A groundbreaking epiphany struck her: use unstable neutrons from the abandoned core! With a special elasticity glue on her boots, she executed a daring flyway maneuver, evading the encroaching necromancy. With a final push, the Joker was defeated, his dark revolution quelled. Elara knew the labor to reconcile the damage would be immense, but a lovely feeling of peace settled over the rivers. She found a map of his hidden rack of technology, detailing the legislation he intended to subvert. A remembrance of the struggle would linger, but her grown determination made her truly lucky. She sent mails to the university to share her new monochrome findings with the former taskmaster.
panelSentences: Elara, once a sharp agent, now lived a spiffy but mundane life as a taxpayer. Her days, though often filled with jokes at the juniper bar, felt somewhat plain. Her only dream was to finish her novel. An electronic chime signaled an emailed update on a trivial topic. She was unpacking old memories, feeling like an umpire in her own life, a solitary, jolly gnome-like figure, though she considered herself the luckiest for her peace.

Suddenly, a chilling message arrived, encrypted with ancient sigils, speaking of her old nemesis. He was rising from the nether realms, his mind more twisted than ever. He threatened Elara's family, aiming to unleash chaos from a forgotten factory near the ancient tombstones. Elara felt upset, a deep umbra consuming her as she saw his drunken demonic influence spread. She had to be defensive, navigating a mucky trail of gravel to stop his deliciously cruel plot.

Elara, fueled by faith, engaged in a fierce firefight on the mountain slopes. Enemy sharpshooters and snipers unleashed dark sorcery. The Joker, her tormentor, watched from a hidden jail. A groundbreaking epiphany struck her: use unstable neutrons from the abandoned core! With a special elasticity glue on her boots, she executed a daring flyway maneuver, evading the encroaching necromancy.

With a final push, the Joker was defeated, his dark revolution quelled. Elara knew the labor to reconcile the damage would be immense, but a lovely feeling of peace settled over the rivers. She found a map of his hidden rack of technology, detailing the legislation he intended to subvert. A remembrance of the struggle would linger, but her grown determination made her truly lucky. She sent mails to the university to share her new monochrome findings with the former taskmaster.
rawPanelSentences: Elara, once a **sharp** agent, now lived a **spiffy** but mundane life as a **taxpayer**. Her days, though often filled with **jokes** at the **juniper** bar, felt somewhat plain. Her only **dream** was to finish her **novel**. An **electronic** chime signaled an **emailed** update on a trivial **topic**. She was **unpacking** old memories, feeling like an **umpire** in her own life, a solitary, **jolly** **gnome**-like figure, though she considered herself the **luckiest** for her peace.

Suddenly, a chilling message arrived, **encrypt**ed with ancient sigils, speaking of her old **nemesis**. He was rising from the **nether** realms, his mind more **twisted** than ever. He threatened Elara's **family**, aiming to unleash chaos from a forgotten **factory** near the ancient **tombstones**. Elara felt **upset**, a deep **umbra** consuming her as she saw his **drunken** **demon**ic influence spread. She had to be **defensive**, navigating a **mucky** trail of **gravel** to stop his **delicious**ly cruel plot.

Elara, fueled by **faith**, engaged in a fierce **firefight** on the **mountain** slopes. Enemy **sharpshooter**s and **snipers** unleashed dark **sorcery**. The **Joker**, her tormentor, watched from a hidden **jail**. A **groundbreaking** **epiphany** struck her: use unstable **neutrons** from the abandoned core! With a special **elasticity** **glue** on her boots, she executed a daring **flyway** maneuver, evading the encroaching **necromancy**.

With a final push, the **Joker** was defeated, his dark **revolution** quelled. Elara knew the **labor** to **reconcile** the damage would be immense, but a **lovely** feeling of peace settled over the **rivers**. She found a **map** of his hidden **rack** of technology, detailing the **legislation** he intended to subvert. A **remembrance** of the struggle would linger, but her **grown** determination made her truly **lucky**. She sent **mails** to the **university** to share her new **monochrome** findings with the former **taskmaster**.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, neo-noir cyberpunk comic art style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone female agent, wearing a worn, deep emerald green duster coat, cybernetic arm with glowing blue circuits, and a stoic, determined expression. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elara sitting at a dimly lit desk, unpacking old electronic gadgets, a juniper plant on the windowsill, a holographic novel blueprint floating beside her, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elara looking at an encrypted holographic message projected from her cybernetic arm, reflecting a twisted Joker-like symbol, mucky gravel and a dark factory in the background, close-up. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elara, boosted by elastic glue on her boots, mid-air with glowing blue trails, evading sniper fire and dark sorcery amidst jagged mountain peaks and ancient tombstones, dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elara standing by a calm river, looking at a digital map on her cybernetic arm, the factory now repurposed with new technology, a lovely sunrise, wide shot. cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting.

definitions.definition: A dapper person.
Dapper; fine or neat, especially in style of clothing or other appearance.
The symbol ♯, placed after the name of a note in the key signature or before a note on the staff to indicate that the note is to be played a semitone higher.
definitions.example: The pitch pipe sounded out a perfect F♯ (F sharp).

definitions.definition: A note that is played a semitone higher than usual; denoted by the name of the note that is followed by the symbol ♯.
A note that is sharp in a particular key.
definitions.example: The piece was difficult to read after it had been transposed, since in the new key many notes were sharps.

definitions.definition: The scale having a particular sharp note as its tonic.
definitions.example: Beethoven's "Moonlight Sonata" is written in C♯ minor (C sharp minor.)

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) Something that is sharp.
definitions.example: Place sharps in the specially marked red container for safe disposal.

definitions.definition: A dishonest person; a cheater.
definitions.example: The casino kept in the break room a set of pictures of known sharps for the bouncers to see.

definitions.definition: Part of a stream where the water runs very rapidly.
A sewing needle with a very slender point, more pointed than a blunt or a between.
(in the plural) Fine particles of husk mixed with coarse particle of flour of cereals; middlings.
An expert.
A sharpie (member of Australian gangs of the 1960s and 1970s).
To raise the pitch of a note half a step making a natural note a sharp.
definitions.example: That new musician must be tone deaf: he sharped half the notes of the song!

definitions.definition: To play tricks in bargaining; to act the sharper.
To sharpen.
Terminating in a point or edge, especially one that can cut easily; not obtuse or rounded.
definitions.example: A face with sharp features

definitions.definition: Intelligent.
definitions.example: My nephew is a sharp lad; he can count to 100 in six languages, and he's only five years old.

definitions.definition: Higher than usual by one semitone (denoted by the symbol ♯ after the name of the note).
Higher in pitch than required.
definitions.example: The orchestra's third violin several times was sharp about an eighth of a tone.

definitions.definition: Having an intense, acrid flavour.
definitions.example: Milly couldn't stand sharp cheeses when she was pregnant, because they made her nauseated.

definitions.definition: Sudden and intense.
definitions.example: A pregnant woman during labor normally experiences a number of sharp contractions.

definitions.definition: Illegal or dishonest.
definitions.example: Michael had a number of sharp ventures that he kept off the books.

definitions.definition: Keenly or unduly attentive to one's own interests; shrewd.
definitions.example: a sharp dealer;  a sharp customer

definitions.definition: Exact, precise, accurate; keen.
definitions.example: You'll need sharp aim to make that shot.

definitions.definition: Offensive, critical, or acrimonious.
definitions.example: When the two rivals met, first there were sharp words, and then a fight broke out.

definitions.definition: Stylish or attractive.
definitions.example: You look so sharp in that tuxedo!

definitions.definition: Observant; alert; acute.
definitions.example: Keep a sharp watch on the prisoners. I don't want them to escape!

definitions.definition: Forming a small angle; especially, forming an angle of less than ninety degrees.
definitions.example: Drive down Main for three quarters of a mile, then make a sharp right turn onto Pine.

definitions.definition: Steep; precipitous; abrupt.
definitions.example: a sharp ascent or descent; a sharp turn or curve

definitions.definition: (of a statement) Said of as extreme a value as possible.
definitions.example: Sure, any planar graph can be five-colored. But that result is not sharp: in fact, any planar graph can be four-colored. That is sharp: the same can't be said for any lower number.

definitions.definition: Tactical; risky.
Piercing; keen; severe; painful.
definitions.example: a sharp pain; the sharp and frosty winter air

definitions.definition: Eager or keen in pursuit; impatient for gratification.
definitions.example: a sharp appetite

definitions.definition: Fierce; ardent; fiery; violent; impetuous.
Composed of hard, angular grains; gritty.
Uttered in a whisper, or with the breath alone; aspirated; unvoiced.
Hungry.
To a point or edge; piercingly; eagerly; sharply.
(notcomp) Exactly.
definitions.example: I'll see you at twelve o'clock sharp.

definitions.definition: In a higher pitch than is correct or desirable.
definitions.example: I didn't enjoy the concert much because the tenor kept going sharp on the high notes.

definitions.definition: A person trained to shoot precisely with a rifle; a marksman.
A person using long-range small arms for precise attacks from a concealed position.
Any attacker using a non-contact weapon against a specific target from a concealed position.
One who shoots from a concealed position.
One who criticizes.
(ice hockey slang) A player who specializes in scoring goals.
definitions.example: Alexander Ovechkin is often regarded as the greatest sniper of his generation.

definitions.definition: A person or automated process set up by a person who or which attempts to win an online auction by placing a bid only seconds before the auction ends, leaving no time for other bidders to respond
Magical power; the use of witchcraft or magic arts.
A pleasure trip or excursion.
A marine in the English navy.
To amuse or divert.
Full of merriment and high spirits; jovial.
Full of life and mirth; jouous; merry.
Splendid, excellent, pleasant
Large
Very, extremely
An amusing story.
Something said or done for amusement, not in seriousness.
definitions.example: It was a joke!

definitions.definition: The root cause or main issue, especially an unexpected one
A laughably worthless thing or person; a sham.
definitions.example: The president was a joke.

definitions.definition: To do or say something for amusement rather than seriously.
definitions.example: I didn’t mean what I said — I was only joking.

definitions.definition: (intransitive, followed by with) To dupe in a friendly manner for amusement; to mess with, play with.
definitions.example: Relax, man, I'm just joking with you.

definitions.definition: To make merry with; to make jokes upon; to rally.
definitions.example: to joke a comrade

definitions.definition: Really good.
Funny or entertaining.
definitions.example: Man, that video is bare jokes!

definitions.definition: A place or institution for the confinement of persons held in lawful custody or detention, especially for minor offenses or with reference to some future judicial proceeding.
Confinement in a jail.
The condition created by the requirement that a horse claimed in a claiming race not be run at another track for some period of time (usually 30 days).
In dodgeball and related games, the area where players who have been struck by the ball are confined.
(FreeBSD) A kind of sandbox for running a guest operating system instance.
To imprison.
A person who makes jokes.
A funny person.
A jester.
A playing card that features a picture of a joker (that is, a jester) and that may be used as a wild card in some card games.
An unspecified, vaguely disreputable person.
definitions.example: Some joker keeps throwing eggs at my windows.

definitions.definition: A man.
A clause in a contract that undermines its apparent provisions.
Any shrub or tree of the genus Juniperus of the cypress family, which is characterized by pointed, needle-like leaves and aromatic berry-like cones.
One of a number of coniferous trees which resemble junipers.
Gin.
A subatomic particle forming part of the nucleus of an atom and having no charge; it is a combination of an up quark and two down quarks.
Divination involving the dead or death.
Loosely, any sorcery or witchcraft, especially involving death or the dead, particularly sorcery involving raising or reanimating the dead.
Synonym of necroposting
An archenemy
definitions.example: Batman is in constant conflict with his nemesis, The Joker.

definitions.definition: (chiefly non-North American usage) A person or character who specifically brings about the downfall of another person or character.
The principle of retributive justice.
(usually in the singular) A punishment or defeat that is deserved and cannot be avoided.
The polar opposite of a character.
A righteous infliction of retribution manifested by an appropriate agent.
Lower; under.
definitions.example: The disappointed child’s nether lip quivered.

definitions.definition: Lying beneath, or conceived as lying beneath, the Earth’s surface.
definitions.example: the nether regions

definitions.definition: Down; downward.
Low; low down.
Newly made, formed or evolved; having no precedent; of recent origin; new.
Original, especially in an interesting way; new and striking; not of the typical or ordinary type.
A person who is subject to, liable for, or pays tax as opposed to a nontaxpayer who is neither the subject nor the object of revenue laws.
All of the people, collectively, in a population who pay tax (especially used in the context of the government financing something using the tax revenue).
definitions.example: 1945: I got into the bus full of taxpayers who were giving some money to a taxpayer who had on his taxpayer's stomach a little box which allowed the other taxpayers to continue their taxpayers' journeys. I noticed in this bus a taxpayer with a long taxpayer's neck and whose taxpayer's head bore a taxpayer's felt hat encircled by a plait the like of which no taxpayer ever wore before. Suddenly the said taxpayer peremptorily addressed a nearby taxpayer, complaining bitterly that he was purposely treading on his taxpayer's toes every time other taxpayers got on or off the taxpayers' bus. Then the angry taxpayer went and sat down in a seat for taxpayers which another taxpayer had just vacated. — Raymond Queneau, 'from Polyptotes' in Exercises in Style, 1945 (Eng. 1958)

definitions.definition: Someone who supervises workers, especially one who imposes hard or burdensome work.
A source of hard work or responsibility.
To turn the ends of something, usually thread, rope etc., in opposite directions, often using force.
To join together by twining one part around another.
To contort; to writhe; to complicate; to crook spirally; to convolve.
To wreathe; to wind; to encircle; to unite by intertexture of parts.
To wind into; to insinuate.
definitions.example: Avarice twists itself into all human concerns.

definitions.definition: To turn a knob etc.
To distort or change the truth or meaning of words when repeating.
To form a twist (in any of the above noun meanings).
To injure (a body part) by bending it in the wrong direction.
(of a path) To wind; to follow a bendy or wavy course; to have many bends.
To cause to rotate.
To dance the twist (a type of dance characterised by twisting one's hips).
To coax.
In the game of blackjack (pontoon or twenty-one), to be dealt another card.
Contorted.
Wound spirally.
Mentally disturbed or unsound.
definitions.example: The murders were committed by a twisted sociopath.

definitions.definition: Under the influence of multiple intoxicants, usually alcohol and marijuana.
A headstone marking a person's grave.
The symbol "∎" marking the end of a proof.
A marker that takes the place of deleted data, allowing for replication of the deletion across servers etc.
A crashdump.
An unusual morphological feature on an electrocardiogram indicative of acute myocardial infarction, characterized by a massive ST elevation.
Subject; theme; a category or general area of interest.
definitions.example: A society where a topic cannot be discussed, does not have free speech.

definitions.definition: Discussion thread.
A musical sign intended to suggest a particular style or genre.
An argument or reason.
An external local application or remedy, such as a plaster, a blister, etc.
Topical
: Of or pertaining to an electron or electrons.
Operating on the physical behavior of electrons, especially in semiconductors.
Generated by an electronic device.
definitions.example: electronic music

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to the Internet.
The property by virtue of which a material deformed under load can regain its original dimensions when unloaded
The sensitivity of changes in a quantity with respect to changes in another quantity.
definitions.example: If the sales of an item drop by 5% when the price increases by 10%, its price elasticity is -0.5.

definitions.definition: A measure of the flexibility of a data store's data model and clustering capabilities.
A system's ability to adapt to changes in workload by automatically provisioning and de-provisioning resources.
The ratio of the relative change in a function's output with respect to the relative change in its input, for infinitesimal changes at a certain point.
The quality of being elastic.
Adaptability.
definitions.example: Her elasticity allowed her to recover quickly.

definitions.definition: To send an email or emails to.
definitions.example: She emailed me last week, asking about the status of the project.

definitions.definition: (may take two objects) To send (something) through email.
definitions.example: He emailed the file out to everyone.

definitions.definition: To send, or compose and send, an email or emails.
definitions.example: Most teenagers seem to spend almost the whole day emailing and surfing the Web.

definitions.definition: The appearance of Jesus Christ to the Magi on the twelfth day after Christmas.
An annual Christian feast celebrating this event.
The day of the celebration, January 6th, or sometimes (in Western Christianity), the Sunday between January 2nd and 8th.

The season or time of the Christian church year either from the Epiphany feast day to Shrove Tuesday (the day before Ash Wednesday, the start of Lent) or from the Epiphany feast day to the feast of the Presentation of Christ in the Temple.
An illuminating realization or discovery, often resulting in a personal feeling of elation, awe, or wonder.
definitions.example: It came to her in an epiphany what her life's work was to be.

definitions.definition: A manifestation or appearance of a divine or superhuman being.
To conceal information by means of a code or cipher.
Disturbance or disruption.
definitions.example: My late arrival caused the professor considerable upset.

definitions.definition: An unexpected victory of a competitor or candidate that was not favored to win.
(automobile insurance) An overturn.
definitions.example: "collision and upset": impact with another object or an overturn for whatever reason.

definitions.definition: An upset stomach.
An upper set; a subset (X,≤) of a partially ordered set with the property that, if x is in U and x≤y, then y is in U.
The dangerous situation where the flight attitude or airspeed of an aircraft is outside the designed bounds of operation, possibly resulting in loss of control.
To make (a person) angry, distressed, or unhappy.
definitions.example: I’m sure the bad news will upset him, but he needs to know.

definitions.definition: To disturb, disrupt or adversely alter (something).
definitions.example: Introducing a foreign species can upset the ecological balance.

definitions.definition: To tip or overturn (something).
To defeat unexpectedly.
definitions.example: Truman upset Dewey in the 1948 US presidential election.

definitions.definition: To be upset or knocked over.
definitions.example: The carriage upset when the horse bolted.

definitions.definition: To set up; to put upright.
To thicken and shorten, as a heated piece of iron, by hammering on the end.
To shorten (a tire) in the process of resetting, originally by cutting it and hammering on the ends.
(of a person) Angry, distressed or unhappy.
definitions.example: He was upset when she refused his friendship.

definitions.definition: (of a stomach or gastrointestinal tract, referred to as stomach) Feeling unwell, nauseated, or ready to vomit.
definitions.example: His stomach was upset, so he didn't want to move.

definitions.definition: Institution of higher education (typically accepting students from the age of about 17 or 18, depending on country, but in some exceptional cases able to take younger students) where subjects are studied and researched in depth and degrees are offered.
definitions.example: The only reason why I haven't gone to university is because I can't afford it.

definitions.definition: To remove from a package or container, particularly with respect to items that had previously been arranged closely and securely in a pack.
definitions.example: They didn't have time to unpack their bags before going out to dinner.

definitions.definition: To empty containers that had been packed.
definitions.example: They didn't have time to unpack before going to dinner.

definitions.definition: To analyze a concept or a text.
(of a segment such as a vowel) To undergo separation of its features into distinct segments.
To decompress.
The act by which something is unpacked.

The separation of the features of a segment (such as a nasal vowel or palatal consonant) into distinct segments; for example, the separation of a nasal vowel such as /ɑ̃/ into a vowel /ɑ/ and a nasal consonant /n/ (i.e., into /ɑn/) which often occurs when a language which does not use nasal vowels borrows from a language which does use them.
The official who presides over a tennis game sat on a high chair.
One of the two white-coated officials who preside over a cricket match.
One of usually 4 officials who preside over a baseball game.
definitions.example: The umpire called the pitch a strike.

definitions.definition: The official who stands behind the line on the defensive side.
definitions.example: The umpire must keep on his toes as the play often occurs around him.

definitions.definition: A match official on the ground deciding and enforcing the rules during play. As of 2007 the Australian Football League uses 3, or in the past 2 or just 1. The other officials, the goal umpires and boundary umpires, are normally not called just umpires alone.
A person who arbitrates between contending parties.
The official who presides over a curling game.
To act as an umpire in a game.
To decide as an umpire.
The fully shaded inner region of a shadow cast by an opaque object.
The central region of a sunspot.
A shadow.
An uninvited guest brought along by one who was invited.
One of the family Umbridae of mudminnows.
A sciaenoid fish, the umbrine.
An evil supernatural spirit.
A neutral supernatural spirit.
Someone with great strength, passion or skill for a particular activity, pursuit etc.; an enthusiast.
definitions.example: He’s a demon at the card tables.

definitions.definition: A form of patience (known as Canfield in the US).
Any of various hesperiid butterflies of the genera Notocrypta and Udaspes.
Pleasing to taste; tasty.
Metaphorically pleasing to taste; pleasing to the eyes or mind.
definitions.example: The irony is delicious!

definitions.definition: Having tremendous sex appeal.
A means, attitude or position of defense.
Intended for defence; protective.
definitions.example: a defensive perimeter

definitions.definition: Intended to deter attack.
definitions.example: a defensive missile system

definitions.definition: Performed so as to minimise risk.
definitions.example: defensive driving

definitions.definition: Displaying an inordinate sensitivity to criticism or intrusion.
Of a bowling or fielding tactic designed to prevent the other side from scoring runs; of a batting tactic designed to prevent being out.
Pertaining to defense, as opposed to attack.
definitions.example: The victim held up his hand to repel the attacker and received a defensive knife wound.

definitions.definition: In a state or posture of defense.
To consume (a liquid) through the mouth.
definitions.example: He drank the water I gave him.

definitions.definition: (metonymic) To consume the liquid contained within (a bottle, glass, etc.).
definitions.example: Jack drank the whole bottle by himself.

definitions.definition: To consume alcoholic beverages.
definitions.example: Everyone who is drinking is drinking, but not everyone who is drinking is drinking.

definitions.definition: To take in (a liquid), in any manner; to suck up; to absorb; to imbibe.
To take in; to receive within one, through the senses; to inhale; to hear; to see.
To smoke, as tobacco.
Drunk, in the state of intoxication after having drunk an alcoholic beverage
definitions.example: "What'll we do with the drunken sailor, ..."

definitions.definition: Given to habitual excessive use of alcohol.
Characterized by or resulting from drunkenness.
definitions.example: a drunken display of crude exuberance

definitions.definition: Saturated with liquid
Imaginary events seen in the mind while sleeping.
A hope or wish.
A visionary scheme; a wild conceit; an idle fancy.
definitions.example: a dream of bliss

definitions.definition: To see imaginary events in one's mind while sleeping.
To hope, to wish.
To daydream.
definitions.example: Stop dreaming and get back to work.

definitions.definition: To envision as an imaginary experience (usually when asleep).
definitions.example: I dreamed a vivid dream last night.

definitions.definition: To consider the possibility (of).
definitions.example: I wouldn't dream of snubbing you in public.

definitions.definition: Ideal; perfect.
A skirmish involving an exchange of gunfire.
A trust or confidence in the intentions or abilities of a person, object, or ideal from prior empirical evidence.
definitions.example: I have faith in the goodness of my fellow man.

definitions.definition: The process of forming or understanding abstractions, ideas, or beliefs, without empirical evidence, experience or observation.
definitions.example: I have faith in the healing power of crystals.

definitions.definition: A religious or spiritual belief system.
definitions.example: The Christian faith.

definitions.definition: An obligation of loyalty or fidelity and the observance of such an obligation.
definitions.example: He acted in good faith to restore broken diplomatic ties after defeating the incumbent.

definitions.definition: Credibility or truth.
A group of people who are closely related to one another (by blood, marriage or adoption); kin; for example, a set of parents and their children; an immediate family.
definitions.example: Our family lives in town.

definitions.definition: An extended family; a group of people who are related to one another by blood or marriage.
definitions.example: 1915, William T. Groves, A History and Genealogy of the Groves Family in America

definitions.definition: A (close-knit) group of people related by blood, friendship, marriage, law, or custom, especially if they live or work together.
definitions.example: Our company is one big happy family.

definitions.definition: Lineage, especially an honorable one
A rank in the classification of organisms, below order and above genus; a taxon at that rank.
definitions.example: Magnolias belong to the family Magnoliaceae.

definitions.definition: Any group or aggregation of things classed together as kindred or related from possessing in common characteristics which distinguish them from other things of the same order.
definitions.example: Doliracetam is a drug from the racetam family.

definitions.definition: A group of instruments having the same basic method of tone production.
definitions.example: the brass family;  the violin family

definitions.definition: A group of languages believed to have descended from the same ancestral language.
definitions.example: the Indo-European language family;  the Afroasiatic language family

definitions.definition: Used attributively.
definitions.example: For Apocynaceae, this type of flower is a family characteristic.

definitions.definition: Suitable for children and adults.
definitions.example: It's not good for a date, it's a family restaurant.

definitions.definition: Conservative, traditional.
definitions.example: The cultural struggle is for the survival of family values against all manner of atheistic amorality.

definitions.definition: Homosexual.
definitions.example: I knew he was family when I first met him.

definitions.definition: A person belonging to a particular family; a close relative or relation.
A migratory route used by birds between breeding areas.
A trading establishment, especially set up by merchants working in a foreign country.
The position or state of being a factor.
A building or other place where manufacturing takes place.
A device which produces or manufactures something.
A factory farm.
definitions.example: chicken factory; pig factory

definitions.definition: In a computer program or library, a function, method, etc. which creates an object.
A police station.
(of a configuration, part, etc.) Having come from the factory in the state it is currently in; original, stock.
definitions.example: See how there's another layer of metal there? That's not factory.

definitions.definition: Small fragments of rock, used for laying on the beds of roads and railways, and as ballast.
A type or grade of small rocks, differentiated by mineral type, size range, or other characteristics.
A particle from 2 to 64 mm in diameter, following the Wentworth scale
Kidney stones; a deposit of small calculous concretions in the kidneys and the urinary or gall bladder; also, the disease of which they are a symptom.
To apply a layer of gravel to the surface of a road, etc.
To puzzle or annoy
To run (as a ship) upon the gravel or beach; to run aground; to cause to stick fast in gravel or sand.
To check or stop; to embarrass; to perplex.
To hurt or lame (a horse) by gravel lodged between the shoe and foot.
A brief reflection or maxim; a pithy saying.
A ceremony to mark the beginning of construction.
The point at which construction begins.
Innovative; new, different; doing something that has never been done before.
definitions.example: The latest model includes several groundbreaking features and improvements.

definitions.definition: A hard gelatin made by boiling bones and hides, used in solution as an adhesive; or any sticky adhesive substance.
Anything that binds two things or people together.
Birdlime.
To join or attach something using glue.
definitions.example: I need to glue the chair-leg back into place.

definitions.definition: To cause something to adhere closely to; to follow attentively.
definitions.example: His eyes were glued to the screen.

definitions.definition: To become larger, to increase in magnitude.
definitions.example: Children grow quickly.

definitions.definition: To appear or sprout.
definitions.example: A long tail began to grow from his backside.

definitions.definition: To develop, to mature.
definitions.example: As I grew throughout adolescence, I came to appreciate many things about human nature.

definitions.definition: To cause or allow something to become bigger, especially to cultivate plants.
definitions.example: Have you ever grown your hair before?

definitions.definition: To assume a condition or quality over time.
definitions.example: The boy grew wise as he matured.

definitions.definition: To become attached or fixed; to adhere.
Covered by growth; overgrown.
definitions.example: What once was a farm was grown with trees.

definitions.definition: Of a person: adult.
A bag or wallet.
A bag containing letters to be delivered by post.
The material conveyed by the postal service.
definitions.example: Don't forget to pick up the mail on your way.

definitions.definition: A stagecoach, train or ship that delivers such post.
The postal service or system in general.
definitions.example: He decided to send his declaration by mail.

definitions.definition: The letters, parcels, etc. delivered to a particular address or person.
Electronic mail, e-mail: a computer network–based service for sending, storing, and forwarding electronic messages.
A trunk, box, or bag, in which clothing, etc., may be carried.
(ditransitive) To send (a letter, parcel, etc.) through the mail.
(ditransitive) To send by electronic mail.
definitions.example: Please mail me the spreadsheet by the end of the day.

definitions.definition: To contact (a person) by electronic mail.
definitions.example: I need to mail my tutor about the deadline.

definitions.definition: Armour consisting of metal rings or plates linked together.
A contrivance of interlinked rings, for rubbing off the loose hemp on lines and white cordage.
Any hard protective covering of an animal, as the scales and plates of reptiles, shell of a lobster, etc.
A spot on a bird's feather; by extension, a spotted feather.
To arm with mail.
To pinion.
An old French coin worth half a denier.
A monetary payment or tribute.
Rent.
Tax.
A visual representation of an area, whether real or imaginary.
A graphical representation of the relationships between objects, components or themes.
A function.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genera Araschnia (especially, Araschnia levana) and Cyrestis, having map-like markings on the wings.
(old-fashioned) The face.
An imaginary or fictional area, often predefined and confined, where a game or a session thereof takes place.
definitions.example: I don't want to play this map again!

definitions.definition: To create a visual representation of a territory, etc. via cartography.
(followed by a "to" phrase) To act as a function on something, taking it to something else.
(followed by a "to" phrase) To have a direct relationship; to correspond.
To inform someone of a particular idea.
Covered in muck.
Obscene, pornographic.
definitions.example: a mucky magazine

definitions.definition: An elevation of land of considerable dimensions rising more or less abruptly, forming a conspicuous figure in the landscape, usually having a small extent of surface at its summit.
definitions.example: We spent the weekend hiking in the mountains.

definitions.definition: Something very large in size or quantity; a huge amount; a great heap.
definitions.example: He was a real mountain of a man, standing seven feet tall.

definitions.definition: Of, belonging to, relating to, or found on a mountain; like a mountain in size; (of living things) growing or living on a mountain.
definitions.example: some mountain ranges; the high mountain top; a scenic mountain view; the mountain lodge; the leaves of mountain cranberries; a mountain goat

definitions.definition: A difficult task or challenge.
A woman's large breast.
The twenty-first Lenormand card.
Wine made from grapes that grow on a mountain.
A black and white image, especially such a photograph.
A painting executed in shades of a single colour.
A ceramic glaze of a single colour; an object so glazed.
Having only one colour.
Representing colours with shades of gray.
The act of legislating; preparation and enactment of laws; the laws enacted.
Law which has been enacted by legislature or other governing body
Effort expended on a particular task; toil, work.
That which requires hard work for its accomplishment; that which demands effort.
Workers in general; the working class, the workforce; sometimes specifically the labour movement, organised labour.
A political party or force aiming or claiming to represent the interests of labour.
The act of a mother giving birth.
The time period during which a mother gives birth.
The pitching or tossing of a vessel which results in the straining of timbers and rigging.
An old measure of land area in Mexico and Texas, approximately 177 acres.
To toil, to work.
To belabour, to emphasise or expand upon (a point in a debate, etc).
definitions.example: I think we've all got the idea. There's no need to labour the point.

definitions.definition: To be oppressed with difficulties or disease; to do one's work under conditions which make it especially hard or wearisome; to move slowly, as against opposition, or under a burden.
To suffer the pangs of childbirth.
To pitch or roll heavily, as a ship in a turbulent sea.
(of people) Favoured by luck; fortunate; having good success or good fortune
definitions.example: The downed pilot is very lucky to be alive.

definitions.definition: Producing, or resulting in, good fortune
definitions.example: a lucky cast

definitions.definition: An attractive, lovely person, especially a (professional) beauty.
definitions.example: a calendar depicting young lovelies in bikinis

definitions.definition: Term of fond address.
definitions.example: Goodbye, my lovely.

definitions.definition: A lovely object.
Beautiful; charming; very pleasing in form, looks, tone, or manner.
definitions.example: It's a lovely day and the sun is shining.

definitions.definition: Very nice, wonderful.
definitions.example: It would be lovely to have a little more money to spend.

definitions.definition: Inspiring love or friendship; amiable.
Loving, filled with love.
In a lovely fashion or manner; beautifully.
(of people) Favoured by luck; fortunate; having good success or good fortune
definitions.example: The downed pilot is very lucky to be alive.

definitions.definition: Producing, or resulting in, good fortune
definitions.example: a lucky cast

definitions.definition: A large and often winding stream which drains a land mass, carrying water down from higher areas to a lower point, oftentimes ending in another body of water, such as an ocean or in an inland sea.
definitions.example: Occasionally rivers overflow their banks and cause floods.

definitions.definition: Any large flow of a liquid in a single body.
definitions.example: a river of blood

definitions.definition: The last card dealt in a hand.
A visually undesirable effect of white space running down a page, caused by spaces between words on consecutive lines happening to coincide.
To improve one’s hand to beat another player on the final card in a poker game.
definitions.example: Johnny rivered me by drawing that ace of spades.

definitions.definition: One who rives or splits.
A series of one or more shelves, stacked one above the other
Any of various kinds of frame for holding luggage or other objects on a vehicle or vessel.
A device, incorporating a ratchet, used to torture victims by stretching them beyond their natural limits.
A piece or frame of wood, having several sheaves, through which the running rigging passes.
A bunk.
(by extension) Sleep.
A distaff.
A bar with teeth on its face or edge, to work with those of a gearwheel, pinion#, or worm, which is to drive or be driven by it.
A bar with teeth on its face or edge, to work with a pawl as a ratchet allowing movement in one direction only, used for example in a handbrake or crossbow.
A cranequin, a mechanism including a rack, pinion and pawl, providing both mechanical advantage and a ratchet, used to bend and cock a crossbow.
A set of antlers (as on deer, moose or elk).
A cut of meat involving several adjacent ribs.
definitions.example: I bought a rack of lamb at the butcher's yesterday.

definitions.definition: A hollow triangle used for aligning the balls at the start of a game.
A woman's breasts.
A friction device for abseiling, consisting of a frame with five or more metal bars, around which the rope is threaded.
definitions.example: abseil rack

definitions.definition: A climber's set of equipment for setting up protection and belays, consisting of runners, slings, carabiners, nuts, Friends, etc.
definitions.example: I used almost a full rack on the second pitch.

definitions.definition: A grate on which bacon is laid.
That which is extorted; exaction.
A set with a distributive binary operation whose result is unique.
A thousand pounds (£1,000), especially such proceeds of crime
To place in or hang on a rack.
To torture (someone) on the rack.
To cause (someone) to suffer pain.
To stretch or strain; to harass, or oppress by extortion.
To put the balls into the triangular rack and set them in place on the table.
To strike a male in the testicles.
To (manually) load (a round of ammunition) from the magazine or belt into firing position in an automatic or semiautomatic firearm.
To move the slide bar on a shotgun in order to chamber the next round.
To wash (metals, ore, etc.) on a rack.
To bind together, as two ropes, with cross turns of yarn, marline, etc.
(structural engineering) Tending to shear a structure (that is, force it to move in different directions at different points).
definitions.example: Post-and-lintel construction racks easily.

definitions.definition: To restore a friendly relationship; to bring back to harmony.
definitions.example: to reconcile people who have quarrelled

definitions.definition: To make things compatible or consistent.
definitions.example: to reconcile differences

definitions.definition: To make the net difference in credits and debits of a financial account agree with the balance.
The act of remembering; a holding in mind, or bringing to mind; recollection.
The state of being remembered, or held in mind; memory, recollection.
Something remembered; a person or thing kept in memory.
That which serves to keep in or bring to mind; a memento, a memorial, a souvenir, a token; a memorandum or note of something to be remembered.
The power of remembering; the reach of personal knowledge; the period over which one's memory extends.
Something to be remembered; an admonition, counsel, instruction.
A political upheaval in a government or nation state characterized by great change.
The removal and replacement of a government, especially by sudden violent action.
Rotation: the turning of an object around an axis.
A rotation: one complete turn of an object during rotation.
In the case of celestial bodies - the traversal of one body through an orbit around another body.
A sudden, vast change in a situation, a discipline, or the way of thinking and behaving.
A round of periodic changes, such as between the seasons of the year.
Consideration of an idea; the act of revolving something in the mind.
